about.title: About the Dutch School Nairobi
about.subtitle: More than 50 years of educational excellence
about.description: The Dutch School has been offering Dutch-language education in Nairobi for more than 50 years. Our small classrooms focus on individual learning needs, while our large, green compound provides a wonderful place to learn and discover.
about.missionText: We provide international, multilingual, and differentiated education to toddlers and primary school students that fully meets the Dutch Core Objectives and the goals of the International Primary Curriculum, enabling our students to develop into open-minded, inquiring, and critical global citizens.
about.visionText: Our vision is to develop children's own identity and talents to make a valuable contribution to a changing world.

testimonial1.quote: The transition from school in the Netherlands was made easy thanks to the smooth progression from the Toddler Group into Groep 1/2, with both classes located close together and familiar routines helping children feel confident and settled. The bilingual programme allows our children to follow the Dutch curriculum whilst IPC in English builds curiosity through engaging activities. The school bus from Lavington is efficient, and the community is very warm.
testimonial1.author: Jenny

testimonial2.quote: My family has had a wonderful experience at the Netherlands School Nairobi. It provides a warm, welcoming, and nurturing environment where every child is appreciated and valued as an individual. We especially appreciate the school's commitment to supporting students with different learning styles and abilities, ensuring that each child receives the attention and encouragement they need to thrive. The school's diverse community and appreciation of different cultures create a rich learning environment that helps children develop respect, understanding, and confidence. Most importantly, my sons genuinely love going to school every day because it is a happy, caring place where they feel safe, included, and excited to learn.
testimonial2.author: Dennis Kinambuga

quickFacts.title: Quick Facts About the Dutch School

q1.question: The Dutch School in a nutshell
q1.answer: The Dutch School is an accredited school in Nairobi, Kenya, with various departments. The bilingual primary school offers Dutch-language and English-language education for children from age 1.5. Established in 1971 and recognized by the Dutch Education Abroad Foundation (NOB), the school combines the Dutch national curriculum with the International Primary Curriculum (IPC).

q2.question: What curriculum does the Dutch School follow?
q2.answer: We follow a bilingual curriculum combining the official Dutch core objectives with the IEYC (International Early Years Curriculum) and IPC (International Primary Curriculum). This ensures students can transition seamlessly to schools in the Netherlands and Belgium or continue their international education pathway in Kenya or elsewhere.

q3.question: What are the school fees at the Dutch School?
q3.answer: Our fees are divided into two groups: Tariff A for families where the employer reimburses school fees, and Tariff B for families paying out of pocket. Contact us for the detailed fee structure.

q4.question: Do students need to speak Dutch to enroll?
q4.answer: No prior Dutch language knowledge is required for our day school department. Our immersive bilingual program supports children at all language levels. In most cases, students speak fluent Dutch in daily use within a year, through the combination of daily exposure and dedicated language instruction.

q5.question: Can students transition to English-language education?
q5.answer:
Yes. Thanks to our bilingual curriculum, IPC lessons taught in English, and frequent English language classes, students can move on to British, American, or other English-language schools. Throughout their school career, students build a strong foundation in both Dutch and English, leaving them well prepared for the next step within an international education environment.

When parents share their plans with us in good time, we are happy to think along about how best to prepare their child for the transition. For students in Group 8 we can also offer targeted support in preparing for (international) secondary education.

hero.title: About the Dutch School
hero.subtitle: Small in Scale, Great in Impact — over 50 years of educational excellence in Kenya

history.title: Our History
history.content: Founded in 1971, the Netherlands School Society has been a cornerstone of Dutch education in East Africa. What started as a small initiative by Dutch-speaking expatriate families has grown into a thriving international community, proudly recognized by the Dutch Education Abroad Foundation.

timeline.1971: School founded by Dutch-speaking expatriate families
timeline.1985: Official recognition by the Dutch government
timeline.2016: Move to our compound in Karen
timeline.2024: Merger with NTC school, to offer a complete program

approach.title: Small in Scale, Great in Impact
approach.content: Since 1971, the Dutch School (formerly Netherlands School Society) has been a trusted place for Dutch and international families in Nairobi. A place where children learn, discover, and grow, in a warm environment where education, the joy of learning, and community come together.
approach.paragraph2: We believe that greatness lies in the small. 'Small in Scale, Great in Impact' captures how our strength lies precisely in the closeness and connection within our school. Because we truly know each other, children feel safe to be themselves and have the space to learn and grow in their own way.
approach.paragraph3: In our small-scale environment, students, teachers, and parents work closely together. We listen to the voice of every child, encourage their personal talents, and celebrate the small moments that matter, from a good conversation over coffee to a grand Sinterklaas celebration or a helping hand when needed.
approach.paragraph4: We offer the best of both worlds: strong bilingual (Dutch/English) education with an international perspective. With the Dutch core objectives and the International Primary Curriculum (IPC), children learn thematically and through discovery, sparking their curiosity and building their self-confidence. Small groups, personal attention, and a close-knit parent community ensure that every child feels seen and valued.
approach.paragraph5: With more than 10 nationalities and cultures within our community, we are proud of both our diversity and our connectedness. Because no matter how far our students later fly, the foundation they build here, in knowledge, in friendship, in the feeling of being together, always stays close.
approach.paragraph6: The Dutch School — Small in Scale, Great in Impact. A place where every child is allowed to grow, and where the small things turn out to be the greatest of all.
approach.quote: The Small Dutch-Speaking School for Great Global Citizens.

team.title: Our Management Team
team.subtitle: A dedicated management team that, with passion and vision, leads our commitment to high-quality education and works alongside the wider team to create an inspiring learning environment where every child can develop to their full potential.
team.intro: At the Dutch School, our class teachers are the heart of the school. They are inspired, Dutch-speaking education professionals with a passion for learning and development. In the lower grades, they are carefully supported by our dedicated English-speaking classroom assistants. Together they create a safe, stimulating environment in which every child can grow.
team.intro2: Our team is our greatest strength: with their expertise, attention, and dedication, they bring out the best in your child's learning every day. We also work with specialized subject teachers for English, swimming, and sports, so every child can develop optimally in these areas as well. These subject teachers bring their passion and expertise to make our education even richer and more varied. For our Skills Clubs and after-school activities, we partner with trusted consultants and experienced experts who contribute with the same engagement to the broad development of our students.

value1.title: Connect
value1.description: We create strong bonds between students, parents, and teachers in a close-knit community.

value2.title: Inspire
value2.description: We spark curiosity and a lifelong love of learning.

value3.title: Move Together
value3.description: We adapt to the unique needs of each child and situation.

value4.title: Global Citizenship
value4.description: We guide students to grow into open, respectful, and curious global citizens who feel connected to the world and contribute to it actively and responsibly.

cta.title: We warmly welcome you to our school community.
cta.subtitle: Schedule a visit to experience our campus and meet our team.

hero.title: Our Vision & Mission
hero.subtitle: Small in Scale, Great in Impact — where every child is seen, known, and valued

philosophy.title: Small Makes Great
philosophy.content: At the Dutch School, we believe that 'small makes great'. In an intimate setting where every child is personally known, we can provide the best environment for development and growth.
philosophy.quote: Being small in scale lets us be great in impact — we excel at what we do precisely because we know each other so well.

mission.title: Our day school mission
mission.content: At the Dutch School, we offer international, multilingual, and differentiated education in which children develop into open-minded, inquiring, and critical global citizens.
mission.paragraph2: We stand for high-quality education, in which students get the unique opportunity to learn and maintain the Dutch and English languages in an international environment. Our goal is for students to grow up bilingual, learn to think creatively and critically, and stay motivated to learn, their whole life long.
mission.paragraph3: We believe that every child has unique talents and potential, which are best discovered, nurtured, and expressed in a small, intimate learning environment with personal attention. We believe in a small school community with close ties between teachers, students, and parents, to give everyone a voice and combine strengths. With the goal of letting students learn, discover, and grow to their optimal individual potential. So they ultimately stand strong on their feet and make a difference in an ever-changing world that reaches beyond the Netherlands, Belgium, and/or Kenya.

vision.title: Our vision
vision.content: We believe in cultivating each person's own identity and talents, so that students find their place in a changing world with confidence and resilience.
vision.paragraph2: Our education challenges students to remain curious, take responsibility for their learning process, and make a positive contribution to their surroundings.
vision.visionQuote: Those who know their own strengths and beautiful imperfections, dare to ask, discover, learn, adapt… to keep growing!

motto.title: Our Motto
motto.subtitle: Bilingual learners, international community, growing together!
motto.content: A motto that captures who we are: a close-knit, international school community in which students grow together; in language, knowledge, and connection.

connect.title: Connect
connect.description: We build strong relationships between school, home, and community. Connection is the foundation of everything we do.

inspire.title: Inspire
inspire.description: We stimulate curiosity, creativity, and a lifelong love of learning in every child.

move.title: Move Together
move.description: We are flexible and adapt to the unique needs of each child and situation.

cta.title: Experience Our Vision in Practice
cta.subtitle: Come for a tour and discover what small-scale education can mean for your child.

hero.title: Our Team
hero.subtitle: Dedicated teachers who work with passion to help every child grow.

intro.title: Teaching Team
intro.content: At the Dutch School, our class teachers are the heart of the school. They are inspired, Dutch-speaking education professionals with a passion for learning and development. In the lower grades, they are carefully supported by our dedicated English-speaking classroom assistants. Together they create a safe, stimulating environment in which every child can grow.
intro.content2: Our team is our greatest strength: with their expertise, attention, and dedication, they bring out the best in your child's learning every day. We also work with specialized subject teachers for English, swimming, and sports, so every child can develop optimally in these areas as well. These subject teachers bring their passion and expertise to make our education even richer and more varied. For our Skills Clubs and after-school activities, we partner with trusted consultants and experienced experts who contribute with the same engagement to the broad development of our students.

wholeTeam.title: The Whole Team
wholeTeam.content: At the Dutch School, we are proud of our close-knit and engaged team. Together we make sure our school is a place where children feel safe, seen, and challenged.
wholeTeam.content2: Our team consists of much more than teachers alone. Every staff member contributes in their own way to the warm and well-organized school life. Our beautiful, green compound is carefully maintained by our gardeners, two of whom also dedicatedly drive the school bus to bring the children safely to and from school.
wholeTeam.content3: Our Finance & Operations Manager watches over the day-to-day running behind the scenes and is supported by our caretaker. Our cleaners ensure every day that the school stays clean, fresh, and welcoming.
wholeTeam.content4: We offer all our staff a safe, respectful working environment in which collaboration, engagement, and joy take center stage. Because only when our team feels good can we together create an optimal learning environment for every child.

leadership.title: School Leadership
leadership.description: Our school leader carries daily responsibility for education and the wellbeing of all students. She works closely with the Learning Support Coordinator, the NTC coordinator, and the Finance and Operations Manager; together they form the Management Team.

teachers.title: Teachers
teachers.description: Our qualified teachers combine Dutch educational experience with international expertise. They create a safe, stimulating learning environment.

support.title: Support Staff
support.description: From teaching assistants, gardeners, cleaners, and bus drivers to administration, our team works together to ensure the best experience for children and parents.

board.title: Board
board.description: Our board consists of volunteer parents elected by the members of the association. All parents with children at the school are automatically members of the association. The board oversees the management team and safeguards the policy and quality of education and school organisation.

cta.title: Meet Our Team
cta.subtitle: Schedule a visit and meet the people who will guide your child.

hero.title: Location & Campus
hero.subtitle: A green oasis in the heart of Karen, Nairobi

location.title: Location
location.content: Dutch School Nairobi is located in the heart of Karen, one of the greenest and most peaceful neighborhoods in the city. Our school sits on a beautiful, spacious compound with plenty of trees, lawns, and shaded spots where children can play and learn safely.
location.paragraph2: The surroundings offer endless play opportunities: running, climbing, playing, football, cycling, or simply enjoying the outdoors. Children get the space, both literally and figuratively, to move, discover, and feel free here.
location.paragraph3: Parents appreciate how easy our school is to reach. The location is central in Karen and easily accessible by car. School transport is also available via our school buses, so families from outside Karen can easily have their children dropped off and picked up.
location.paragraph4: The combination of nature, space, and calm makes Dutch School Nairobi a place where children feel at home and come to school happily!

intro.title: Our Educational Offering
intro.content: Dutch School Nairobi offers a complete educational pathway for children from age 1.5. Our bilingual curriculum combines the best of the Dutch educational system with international curricula.

toddler.title: Toddler Group
toddler.description: A warm, playful environment where toddlers take their first steps in their learning journey. Maximum 15 children, 2-5 mornings per week.

languageClub.description: French (groups 5-8) and Kiswahili (groups 1-8) as additional language offerings.

skillsClub.title: Skills Club

cta.title: Find the Right Program
cta.subtitle: We're happy to help you find the best pathway for your child.

hero.title: Toddler Group
hero.subtitle: A warm, playful, bilingual environment where toddlers grow, discover, and develop through play. Maximum 15 children per morning, 2-5 mornings per week.

intro.title: Toddler Group (Peuterspeelzaal)
intro.content: In our toddler group, our youngest learners discover the world at their own pace, in a safe and familiar environment. We welcome toddlers from one and a half years old, two to five mornings per week, in a small group of maximum fifteen children.
intro.paragraph2: Through play, we work on independence, social skills, and language development. Toddlers learn to put on their own coats, tidy up, take turns, and work together with others; important first steps toward primary school. In conversations, stories, and play, both Dutch and English receive plenty of attention, allowing children to build a rich vocabulary in a natural way.
intro.paragraph3: Movement and discovery are central: during outdoor play, toddler gym, and, weather permitting, even at the weekly toddler swimming with their own teacher. Creativity also gets space. The toddlers experiment with paint, clay, scissors, and paper and learn different techniques.
intro.paragraph4: We carefully follow their development with the KIJK! observation system and work with themes from the International Early Years Curriculum (IEYC). This way, the activities connect with the world of young children and prepare them through play for groups 1 and 2. Toddlers move on to group 0/1 when they turn 4.
intro.paragraph5: In short: our toddler education lays a warm, playful, and solid foundation for a lifetime of learning.

schedule.title: Flexible Mornings
schedule.description: Toddlers can attend 2, 3, 4, or 5 mornings per week.

language.title: Language Exposure
language.description: Natural introduction to Dutch and English through songs, stories, and daily interactions.

play.title: Learning Through Play
play.description: Development through play, creativity, and social interaction with peers.

observation.title: KIJK! Observation System
observation.description: We track each child's development with the KIJK! observation system.

activities.title: Weekly Activities
list: Circle time with songs and stories

practical.title: Practical Information
practical.hours: Opening hours: 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM (option until 3:00 PM)
practical.staff: Supervision: experienced pedagogical staff and teaching assistants

items.quote: The Dutch School Nairobi provided a warm and supportive environment for our bilingual children from day one. The teachers took the time to understand each child's unique personality and supported their transition with great care and patience. Thanks to their thoughtful approach, both of our children have grown into confident, happy, and playful learners who truly enjoy going to school.
items.author: Burcu

cta.title: Want to Learn More?
cta.subtitle: Schedule a visit to experience our toddler group.

hero.title: Primary School
hero.subtitle: Fully bilingual education with the Dutch curriculum and the International Primary Curriculum (IPC). Small classes, personal attention.

intro.title: The Best of Both Worlds
intro.content: Our primary school combines the Dutch curriculum with the International Primary Curriculum (IPC). Children receive education in both Dutch and English, preparing them optimally for either return to the Netherlands or continuation of international education.
intro.visionContent: At the Dutch School, we believe that multilingualism is a strength, a key to connection, understanding, and success in an international world. We offer education in which Dutch and English are used in balance, so our students are firmly rooted in Dutch and Flemish culture and can communicate confidently in an international environment.
intro.visionParagraph2: Our bilingual education is more than receiving lessons in two languages. It is a thoughtful approach in which students learn language through meaningful experiences, within rich themes from the International Early Years and Primary Curriculum (IEYC/IPC). This way, children grow into true global citizens: flexible in language, culture, and thinking.
intro.visionParagraph3: Through this approach, students develop not only two strong language systems, but also a deeper language awareness (metalinguistic awareness). They learn to think about language, recognize differences, and use strategies that strengthen their learning capacity. This stimulates their creative, social, and cognitive development, and prepares them for diverse onward education paths; both internationally and Dutch-speaking.
intro.visionParagraph4: Our teachers work purposefully on rich knowledge and skills, with attention to play, inquiry, reflection, and self-insight. Students discover how their brain works, learn how they themselves influence their growth, and experience the joy of learning in two languages.
intro.visionParagraph5: The strength of our bilingual education lies in the balance between familiar and international: Dutch as a solid foundation, English as a window to the world. This way we create an environment in which students can be themselves, experience connection with others, and grow with confidence into multilingual, curious, and open-minded global citizens.

kindergarten.title: Kindergarten Education (Groups 1-2)
kindergarten.description: In our kindergarten groups, we lay a solid foundation for lifelong learning through play. In a rich, bilingual learning environment (3 days Dutch, 2 days English) full of play, discovery, and wonder, children grow in language, self-confidence, and social skills. Through meaningful IEYC themes, we stimulate curiosity, creativity, and a natural development in both Dutch and English.
kindergarten.extended: Dutch-speaking kindergarten education is internationally recognized as powerful and future-focused. In our kindergarten groups, we lay a solid foundation for lifelong learning. Young learning is discovering, wondering, and growing, and that is what our kindergarteners do every day in a rich learning environment full of play, language, and curiosity. Our education is bilingual: three days a week in Dutch and two days fully in English. This way, the children build a natural foundation in both languages, without separate language lessons. Our kindergarten classes are set up with various corners full of play, learning, and development materials, inviting discovery and collaboration. We work with themes from the International Early Years Curriculum (IEYC) and devote much attention to social-emotional development. We track development with the KIJK! observation system. Our kindergarten class is a safe, cheerful, and stimulating place where every child has the space to grow; in language, in self-confidence, and in wonder.

lower.title: Pedagogical and Didactic Approach
lower.description: At the Dutch School, the development of the child is central, both as a student and as a person. In a safe, warm, and structured environment, children learn not only what they learn, but also how they learn. Our teachers stimulate curiosity, self-confidence, and ownership, with attention to executive functions, talent development, and getting to know their own brain. Together we build brain-friendly, future-oriented education in which every child blossoms.
lower.extended: At the Dutch School, we believe that children only truly learn when they feel safe, seen, and valued. Our teachers play a central role in this. They are not only teachers, but also guides in the broad development of every child, with attention to head, heart, and hands. Within our heterogeneous groups, students naturally learn to work together, take responsibility, and consider one another. The basis of our pedagogical approach is shaped by our five golden rules: 1. We make sure everyone feels safe at school. 2. We are friendly and kind to one another. 3. We take good care of our own and others' belongings. 4. We let each other play and work undisturbed. 5. We trust each other. Using the brain powers from Breinhelden, the personal goals of the IPC, and a talent-focused lens, we strengthen not only knowledge but also character. Our education is purposeful, child-centered, and inquiry-based.

upper.title: Broad Development
upper.description: At the Dutch School, we look beyond just academic performance. We guide children in their cognitive, social, creative, and physical development, so they can grow with confidence, resilience, and curiosity. With attention to ownership, talent development, and global citizenship, we create an environment in which every child learns with head, heart, and hands.
upper.extended: At the Dutch School, we believe learning goes beyond knowledge alone. We guide children in their broad development; cognitively, socially, creatively, and physically, so they meet the world with confidence and curiosity. Our teachers stimulate students to think independently, collaborate, and reflect. Within IPC education, personal goals such as resilience, empathy, respect, and responsibility are central. Programs like Breinhelden help children understand their emotions, stay focused, and persevere; qualities that contribute to growth, self-confidence, and joy in learning. Alongside a strong academic program, we offer space for creativity and talent development. During our Skills Clubs, students discover new interests in art, music, sport, woodworking, and gardening. Movement is also a regular part of the weekly program, with weekly gym and swimming lessons for all groups. As an international Dutch-speaking school in a diverse community, we devote much attention to citizenship and global citizenship.

assessment.title: Assessment & Tracking
assessment.content: We use observation and student monitoring systems to track each child's progress. In group 8, students take the official transition test.
items: CITO student monitoring system for groups 1-8, benchmarked with the Netherlands

hero.title: NTC - Dutch Language and Culture
hero.subtitle: For Dutch-speaking children at other international schools

intro.title: Maintain and Develop Dutch
intro.content: The NTC program is specially designed for children who attend another international school but want to maintain and develop their Dutch language and culture. Perfect for expat families who will later return to the Netherlands or Belgium.

forWhom.title: For Whom?
forWhom.content: The NTC program is intended for children of Dutch or Belgian families who:
items: Attend another international school in Nairobi
Want to maintain or improve their Dutch
Want to prepare for return to the Netherlands/Belgium
Want to stay connected to Dutch culture and traditions

culture.title: Dutch Culture

schedule.title: Flexible Schedule
schedule.description: Lessons after school or on Wednesday afternoon.

hero.title: Our Approach
hero.subtitle: A balanced bilingual education program with IEYC, IPC, Dutch core objectives, and English language lessons

intro.title: The Best of Both Systems
intro.content: Our curriculum combines the strengths of the Dutch educational system with the internationally recognized IEYC and IPC. This ensures broad, balanced development of every child.

english.title: English Lessons
english.intro: Language connects, opens worlds, and brings people closer together, and that is exactly what we want to achieve at the Dutch School with our bilingual education.
english.paragraph2: We offer all students from groups 3-8 English language lessons twice a week, taught by a native English speaker. In these lessons, listening, speaking, reading, and writing come together in a lively and meaningful way.
english.paragraph3: We use the Pearson Now I Know method, combined with the Bilingual Primary Education learning line for English, with which we build strong communicative skills and a solid grammatical foundation. Our ambitions are high: many students achieve, by the end of group 8, an English language level comparable to HAVO 4 in the Netherlands. That ensures a smooth transition to international education, which a large portion of our students continue after completing primary school.
english.paragraph4: Within our IEYC/IPC lessons, English is also used as the language of instruction. This helps students learn to use the language naturally across diverse subject areas, from science to art and society. At the same time, Dutch remains firmly anchored in our curriculum, so students learn to switch between languages and strengthen their metalinguistic awareness.
english.paragraph5: In addition to Dutch and English, students get the chance to discover other languages. In our Language Club, we offer French and Kiswahili, languages that connect with both our students' backgrounds and our international and local environment in Kenya.
english.paragraph6: This way, our students grow step by step into confident, multilingual global citizens who feel at home in multiple languages and cultures.

dutch.title: Dutch Curriculum
dutch.content: The core of our education is based on Dutch core objectives and reference levels.

language.title: Dutch Language
language.description: Reading, writing, speaking, and listening according to Dutch standards.

reading.title: Reading Comprehension
reading.description: Development of reading strategies and text understanding.

ieyc.title: IEYC (Early Years)
ieyc.content: For our youngest learners, we use the International Early Years Curriculum.

ipc.title: IPC (Primary Curriculum)
ipc.content: The International Primary Curriculum offers thematic, inquiry-based learning.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.content: We monitor student progress with recognized assessment instruments.

cta.title: More Information?
cta.subtitle: We'd be happy to tell you more about our curriculum.

hero.title: Language Club
hero.subtitle: Learn additional languages beyond Dutch and English

intro.title: Expand Language Skills
intro.content: In addition to our bilingual Dutch-English program, we offer extra language lessons. Children can learn French or Kiswahili, perfect for their international future and local integration.
intro.paragraph2: Our Language Club gives students 45 minutes a week to deepen and broaden their language skills. We focus on the four languages that play a role in our school: Dutch, English, French, and Kiswahili.
intro.paragraph3: For students who do not yet fully master Dutch or English, the Language Club offers extra language support. This way, they build a solid foundation for the rest of their school career. Other students can choose Kiswahili (groups 1-8) or French (groups 5-8).
intro.paragraph4: In Kiswahili lessons, students work playfully on listening and speaking skills around familiar themes such as greetings, counting, family, animals, food, and visiting the market. We work with different level groups, from beginner to advanced, so every child progresses at their own pace.
intro.paragraph5: The French language club starts from group 5 with a playful introduction to the language and culture. In groups 7 and 8, we work toward the final goals of Flemish education for year 6. This way, Flemish students can further strengthen their home language, while other students lay a solid foundation in a third European language.
intro.paragraph6: The goal of the Language Club is to offer children a rich language program, increase their language enjoyment and cultural curiosity, and prepare them for various future learning paths. We see multilingualism as a strength: it stimulates creative thinking, strengthens social skills, and enriches learning at all levels.
intro.paragraph7: We are proud of our Language Club: a place where languages come to life and students grow in communication and self-confidence.

french.title: French
french.content: French lessons as preparation for education in Belgium and secondary education.

kiswahili.title: Kiswahili
kiswahili.content: Kiswahili lessons for connection with the local community and culture.

schedule.title: Schedule
schedule.content: The extra language lessons as part of the Language Club are given during school time, integrated into the weekly schedule, at least once a week.

cta.title: Interested in Extra Languages?
cta.subtitle: Ask about the possibilities for your child.

hero.title: Library
hero.subtitle: A beloved place where the joy of reading takes center stage

intro.title: Library
intro.content: At the Dutch School, the joy of reading is central. We believe it is important that every child discovers the magic of stories, not only to learn language, but most of all to enjoy reading itself.
intro.paragraph2: Because there is no Dutch library around the corner in Kenya, we cherish our own extensive collection of Dutch-language children's books, supplemented with informative titles and a fine selection of English books. The latter are used during English lessons and to further encourage reading at home. Every year we expand our library with new, current books, so there is always something inspiring to discover.
intro.paragraph3: The library is a beloved place in our school. Here children learn not only to read, but also to take care of their books. With their own canvas library bag, they choose new books every week, thanks to our dedicated 'library parents' who keep everything running smoothly.
intro.paragraph4: Throughout the year, we highlight various themes and book genres. A highlight is the Children's Book Week in October, during which the whole school buzzes with stories, reading aloud, and creative book activities for two weeks.
intro.paragraph5: Our library brings not only children but also parents closer to the joy of reading; an invitation to keep discovering, browsing, and enjoying the world that books open up together.

images.image1Alt: Children reading in the school library
images.image2Alt: Child using a digital reading device in the library

cta.title: Come Visit Our Library
cta.subtitle: Schedule a tour and discover our rich collection of books.

hero.title: Skills Club
hero.subtitle: Activities during school hours for broad development

intro.title: More than cognitive learning
intro.content: The Skills Club offers, twice a week during school hours and as part of the learning program, a rich variety of activities. Students discover and develop their talents, practical skills, creativity, and social competencies in an inspiring environment in which joy and engagement take center stage.
intro.paragraph2: Twice a week, our students close their school day with something special: the Skills Club. During these afternoons, children get the chance to discover their talents, develop new skills, and most of all to learn with joy.
intro.paragraph3: The Skills Clubs offer a wide range of activities, from art, music, and sport to woodworking and gardening. By learning with their hands, heart, and head, students discover where their strength lies and dare to take on new challenges. The emphasis is not on grades or performance, but on personal growth, creativity, and collaboration.
intro.paragraph4: Within the clubs, students learn skills that reach beyond the classroom: perseverance, responsibility, communication, and self-confidence. Step by step, they develop the executive functions and social skills that help them stand independently, resiliently, and curiously in life.
intro.paragraph5: The Skills Clubs are led by trusted consultants and experienced experts who love to share their passion with the children. This way, we offer an inspiring learning environment in which every child has the space to grow, in their own way.
intro.paragraph6: At the Dutch School, we believe that learning goes beyond books and tests. In the Skills Club, students discover what they can do, what they enjoy, and who they want to become.

art.title: Art & Crafts
art.description: Drawing, painting, crafting, and other creative expressions.

woodwork.title: Woodworking
woodwork.description: Practical skills with wood and tools.

gardening.title: Gardening
gardening.description: Working in the school garden, learning about nature and food.

digital.title: Digital Literacy
digital.description: Creative and inquiry-based use of technology, media, and digital skills.

schedule.title: When?
schedule.content: Skills Club activities take place during school hours. There is a fixed offering throughout the school year and a number of rotating activities per term.

cta.title: What Activities Are Available Now?
cta.subtitle: Ask about the current Skills Club offerings.

hero.title: Student Support
hero.subtitle: Personal guidance for every child

intro.title: Every Child is Unique
intro.content: In our small-scale school, we know every child personally. Our student support is focused on supporting each child's development, with extra attention for those who need it.

ib.title: Learning Support Coordinator
ib.description: Our learning support coordinator coordinates student support, assists teachers, and guides children who need extra help.

observation.title: Observation & Signaling
observation.description: We systematically observe children to identify early when extra support is needed.

support.title: Appropriate Support
support.description: From extra instruction to classroom adaptations - we look for solutions that work for each child.

parents.title: Partnership with Parents
parents.description: Parents are partners in guiding their child. We share observations and align approaches.

assessment.title: Tracking Systems
assessment.content: We use recognized systems to track children's development and compare with Dutch standards.

kijk.title: KIJK!
kijk.description: Observation system for toddlers and kindergarteners that maps broad development.

cito.title: CITO LVS
cito.description: The Dutch student monitoring system for testing language and mathematics, comparable to schools in the Netherlands.

doorstroom.title: Transition Test
doorstroom.description: Official Dutch test in group 8 for advice toward secondary education.

pearson.title: Pearson English Benchmark
pearson.description: International test for measuring English language proficiency.

gifted.title: Giftedness
gifted.content: Children who need more challenge also receive appropriate attention from us. We offer enrichment and deepening where needed.

cta.title: Questions About Student Support?
cta.subtitle: We'd be happy to tell you more about how we guide children.

hero.title: Practical Information
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about school life

intro.title: Well Prepared
intro.content: On these pages, you'll find all practical information about school hours, transport, and tuition. Have questions? Feel free to contact us.

schedule.title: School Hours
schedule.description: Information about lesson times, breaks, and holidays.

transport.title: Transport
transport.description: Our school buses run various routes through Nairobi.

fees.title: School Fees
fees.description: Information about fees and payment arrangements.

food.title: Food & Drink
food.description: Snacks, lunch and eating times together as a class.

contact.title: Questions?
contact.content: For practical questions, you can contact our office.

hero.title: Food & Drink
hero.subtitle: Snacks, lunch and eating times together as a class

intro.content: At the Dutch School, children enjoy eating together with their class. Every day, students bring their own snack and lunch from home, so they can have something they like and that suits them.

schedule.title: Breaks and meal times
schedule.content: The snack is eaten in the classroom, just before the short break from 10:00 to 10:30 AM. Lunch is at 11:45 AM for the lower grades and at 12:15 PM for the middle and upper grades. After that, it's time to play outside and relax.

healthy.title: Healthy choices
healthy.content: We encourage healthy choices. A piece of fruit or another healthy snack is ideal for the morning. At lunchtime many children enjoy a sandwich, but bringing a different meal is also fine. At our school we see a wonderful mix of lunch habits: from chapati to pasta or rice dishes in insulated containers. Diverse, international, and matching what children enjoy and are used to at home. Please note that we cannot heat meals at school.

water.title: Drinking water
water.content: Plenty of fresh drinking water is available for the children to use throughout the day.

social.title: A pleasant moment together
social.content: The snack and lunch moments are meant as a calm and pleasant time together. Often the teacher reads aloud a fun, exciting, or funny story during the meal. A welcome pause in the middle of the school day!

cta.title: Questions about food and drink?
cta.subtitle: Contact us for more information.

hero.title: School Hours
hero.subtitle: Lesson times and annual calendar

toddler.title: Toddler Group
toddler.note: 2-5 mornings of choice. Afternoon care available from 12:00 PM - 3:00 PM (Mon-Thu) and 12:00 PM - 2:00 PM (Fri).

breaks.title: Breaks
breaks.note: Students bring a snack and lunch from home.

calendar.title: School Calendar
calendar.content: Our school year largely follows the Dutch school year, with adjustments for Kenyan holidays. The annual calendar is shared at the beginning of each school year.
calendar.notAvailable: The school calendar for the next school year is not yet available. Please check back closer to the start of the new year.

afterSchool.title: After School
afterSchool.content: After the regular school day we offer After School Activities. These optional activities give children the chance to discover new interests, develop their talents further and enjoy time together outside the classroom. Participation in these elective activities is subject to an additional fee.

cta.title: Questions About School Hours?
cta.subtitle: Contact us for more information.

hero.title: School Transport
hero.subtitle: Safe transportation to and from school

intro.title: Our School Buses
intro.content: Dutch School Nairobi offers school bus transport from various parts of Nairobi. Our two routes cover the main areas where our families live.

route1.title: Route 1: Lavington / Kilimani
route1.description: This route serves the Lavington, Kilimani, and surrounding areas.

route2.title: Route 2: Loresho / Kitisuru
route2.description: This route serves the Loresho, Kitisuru, and surrounding areas.

safety.title: Safety
safety.content: Your child's safety is our priority. Our buses are equipped with seat belts and driven by experienced drivers. A supervisor is present during every trip.

practical.title: Practical Information
items: Pick-up and drop-off times are communicated at the beginning of the school year
Registration for bus transport goes through the school office
Bus transport is optional and separately priced

cta.title: Interested in School Transport?
cta.subtitle: Contact us for availability and rates.

hero.title: School Fees
hero.subtitle: Transparent fee structure

intro.title: Tariff Groups
intro.content: To keep education accessible to as many families as possible, we work with several tariffs. The toddler group has its own pricing based on 2 to 5 mornings per week. Within primary school we distinguish between groups 1-2 and groups 3-8. In addition, we offer an adjusted tariff for families who don't receive a 75% or higher employer contribution.

tariffA.title: Tariff 1
tariffA.description: For families where 75% or more of the school fees are paid by the employer.

tariffB.title: Tariff 2
tariffB.description: For families where less than 75% of the school fees are paid by the employer. Discounts apply for second and third children.

toddlerSection.title: Toddler Tariffs
toddlerSection.subtitle: Per-term rates based on number of mornings per week
notes: Lesson hours with the Dutch-speaking teacher are 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon.
There is an option to stay in the afternoon until 3:00 PM under the supervision of an English-speaking teaching assistant. This is charged at 5 euro per afternoon.
Upon admission a 250 euro deposit is required per toddler. No entry fee is charged for toddlers.

included.title: Included
items: Educational activities and excursions (excluding school camp)
Use of the compound and swimming pool on weekends

payment.title: Payment
payment.content: Tuition can be paid per term or per year. Monthly payment arrangements can be discussed.

siblings.title: Discount
siblings.content: For primary school, a discount applies for the second and subsequent children from the same family.

cta.title: Request Rates
cta.subtitle: Contact us for current rates and a personal conversation.

hero.title: Community
hero.subtitle: A close-knit, international school family

intro.title: Stronger Together
intro.content: The Dutch School is more than a school, it's a community. Parents, students, and team members together form a close-knit group where everyone knows and supports each other.
intro.paragraph2: Every morning, coffee and tea are ready for parents, so after dropping off the children there is space to meet, catch up, and make new connections. This is how we foster a warm and connected school community in which parents easily find one another.
intro.paragraph3: The school is an association, which means that all parents automatically become members of the school community. As members, parents have voting rights and actively help shape the school's future. An engaged board, made up of parents, oversees the running of the school and supports the direction and continuity of the organization.
intro.paragraph4: Twice a year we organize a General Members' Meeting (ALV), in which parents are kept up to date on developments within the school and can vote on important topics such as budgets, annual plans, and other policy choices. Together we build a strong, transparent, and engaged school community.

parents.title: For Parents
parents.description: Parent involvement, communication, and how you can contribute to our school.

events.title: Events
events.description: Dutch and Flemish traditions, theme weeks, and social activities.

cta.title: Become Part of Our Community
cta.subtitle: Come by and experience the warm atmosphere of our school.

hero.title: For Parents
hero.subtitle: Partners in your child's education

intro.title: Parent Involvement
intro.content: At the Dutch School, parents are important partners. We believe in open communication and active parent involvement in school life.

communication.title: Communication
items: Parent App for updates and practical information

involvement.title: Get Involved
involvement.content: There are many ways to be involved:

parentCommittee.title: Coffee Mornings
parentCommittee.content: At the Dutch School we regularly organize coffee mornings to bring parents together and create space for meeting, inspiration, and exchange. During these gatherings we discuss educational themes as well as topics around parenting, child development, and family life.
parentCommittee.paragraph2: The coffee mornings offer a platform to gain new insights, share experiences, and learn from each other. The school regularly puts topics on the table and invites outside experts where appropriate. At the same time we encourage parents to bring their own ideas, or to host a session themselves drawing on their own knowledge, background, or expertise. Together we build a learning and engaged school community.

cta.title: Questions About Parent Involvement?
cta.subtitle: We'd be happy to tell you more.

hero.title: Events
hero.subtitle: Dutch and Flemish traditions and international celebrations

intro.title: A Year Full of Events
intro.content: At the Dutch School we celebrate both Dutch and Flemish traditions and international holidays. These events strengthen our community and connect children with their own cultural background and that of their classmates.

sinterklaas.title: Sinterklaas
sinterklaas.description: In late November / early December the whole school celebrates Sinterklaas with songs, surprises, and pepernoten. The weeks beforehand are also dedicated to the build-up in the classroom.

koningsspelen.title: Koningsspelen and Orange Party
koningsspelen.description: Every year the school takes part in the Koningsspelen, an active sports day. Around King's Day we also organize an orange party with games, a flea market, and Dutch gezelligheid.

belgischeDag.title: Belgian Day
belgischeDag.description: In November we celebrate our annual Belgian Day at school. Students learn more about Belgium, get to know Belgian traditions, and enjoy typical treats and fun activities together. A lovely way to experience culture and celebrate together.

kerst.title: Christmas Celebration
kerst.description: A joint Christmas celebration with performances by the children.

eindmusical.title: End-of-Year Musical
eindmusical.description: Celebrating the end of the school year with a musical and a farewell to group 8 and other departing students.

kinderboekenweek.title: Children's Book Week
kinderboekenweek.description: During the National Children's Book Week the whole school is dedicated to the joy of reading and stories. Around the annual theme we organize activities involving books, reading aloud, and creative language use, so that students discover how fun and inspiring reading can be.

lentekriebels.title: Spring Buzz Week
lentekriebels.description: During the Week of Spring Buzz we give age-appropriate and respectful attention to themes such as relationships, resilience, boundaries, feelings, and learning to understand yourself and others. This supports students' social-emotional development and contributes to a safe and open school culture.

neuroDiversity.title: Neurodiversity Week
neuroDiversity.description: During Neurodiversity Week we celebrate the different ways people think, learn, and experience the world. Students discover that differences are valuable and learn to look at themselves and others with curiosity, understanding, and respect.

wereldReligie.title: World Religions Week
wereldReligie.description: During World Religions Week students get to know various world religions and worldviews. By exploring together, asking questions, and discovering similarities and differences, we encourage respect, openness, and global citizenship.

verkeersweek.title: Traffic Week
verkeersweek.description: During Traffic Week students work on traffic skills and traffic awareness in an active, hands-on way. With games, assignments, and their own bike or other wheels, they learn to apply traffic rules and take part safely in traffic.

calendar.title: Events Calendar
calendar.content: The full events calendar is shared at the beginning of each school year. Parents receive timely invitations for all events.

cta.title: Come to Our Events
cta.subtitle: Experience the warmth of our community.
